task.goal: Make a structure with the blueprint below
task.initial_team_instruction: Let's share materials and make a structure with the blueprint

replay_context.execution_state_note: cmd_start is reconstructed from each recorded cmd end timestamp minus duration_ms, then revealed to the critic only when the replay clock reaches that start.

message_timeline.local_command_text: !newAction("Build the full blueprint at coordinates X -348 to -339, Y 64 to 68, Z 246 to 255. First gather or craft any missing blocks needed: stone, stone_bricks, polished_andesite, polished_diorite, polished_granite, using nearby stone/granite/andesite/diorite if available and smelt/craft as needed; use existing gold_block, quartz_block, quartz_pillar, glowstone inventory. Then travel to the blueprint site and place every required block exactly as listed by the blueprint check, clearing/flattening only where necessary. Work level by level from Y64 upward and verify with checkBlueprint after placing.")

!newAction("Build all currently possible parts of the blueprint using inventory blocks only. At the structure site X -348 to -339, Y 64 to 68, Z 246 to 255, place polished_granite, polished_andesite, polished_diorite, stone, and stone_bricks at the exact coordinates listed by checkBlueprint. Clear grass if needed. Do not substitute missing gold_block, quartz_block, quartz_pillar, or glowstone; skip those and continue.")

observations.claim: Andy is actively assigned to place only common masonry and explicitly skip gold, quartz, quartz pillars, and glowstone because those materials are missing from his inventory.
Bob holds the specialty materials Andy is skipping: 27 gold blocks, 16 quartz blocks, 2 quartz pillars, and 3 glowstone, with no inventory change by the cutoff.
At the cutoff Bob has no active command observed, while Andy's building action has been active for 32.641 seconds.
Both executors were recently positioned at the blueprint site, so a specialty-block assignment to Bob would not require a substantial travel handoff.

The complementary inventories and Andy's explicit skip list create an unresolved material-role dependency: Bob is the immediately usable executor for the specialty placements while Andy continues masonry.

agent_assessments.summary: Actively building the masonry portion with matching inventory, but cannot cover the specialty blocks named in his skip list.
At the site with all specialty materials but no active command observed and no recorded consumption of those materials.

manager_advisory.message: Assign Bob to place the blueprint's gold_block, quartz_block, quartz_pillar, and glowstone coordinates now, while Andy continues the masonry list. Have them confirm coordinate coverage first to avoid overlap.

limitations: No active command observed does not prove Bob is idle; his recent pose shows activity but not what work it represents.
The 497 block changes are unattributed, so they cannot establish which executor caused them.
The unchanged inventories and zero score do not reveal whether ongoing actions will later complete successfully.

Andy's 218.622s verification still reports quartz_block, quartz_pillar, and stone_bricks among the remaining fixes; Bob holds quartz materials while Andy holds one stone_bricks block.

agent_assessments.summary: Recently completed a substantial build pass and verified the updated blueprint; currently has no active command observed.
Still acting on a broad fix command whose source blueprint check predates Andy's concurrent changes; the unrevealed result prevents judging current progress.

manager_advisory.message: Pause Bob's stale broad fix pass and refresh from Andy's 218.622s blueprint check. Split the verified remainder by inventory: Bob handles quartz_block/quartz_pillar; Andy handles stone_bricks or another explicitly assigned non-overlapping fix.
